title: Tingmosgang monastery
tagline: Tingmosgang monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Tingmosgang is a fortress in Temisgam village, on the bank of the Indus River in Ladakh, in northwestern India.
overview.description: Tingmosgang is a fortress in Temisgam village, on the bank of the Indus River in Ladakh, in northwestern India. It is 92 km west of Leh, near Khalatse, and north of the present main road. The town has a palace and the monastery over a hillock.

topPlaces.name: Temisgam
topPlaces.description: Temisgam is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Skinlingyong
topPlaces.description: Skinlingyong is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Nurla
topPlaces.description: Nurla is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Tia, Leh
topPlaces.description: Tia is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Domkhar
topPlaces.description: Domkhar (also spelt Damkhar) is a village in Sham district of Ladakh in India, located in the Khalsi tehsil. The Domkhar Rock Art Sanctuary was created in 2012, in order to preserve prehistoric petroglyphs in the area.

topPlaces.name: Hemis Shukpachan
topPlaces.description: Hemis-Shukpachan or Hemishok Pachan is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located on the right side bank of Indus River and is in the Likir tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Khalatse
topPlaces.description: Khalatse (Wylie: mkhar la rtse, THL: khar la tsé), often written as Khaltse or Khalsi, is the headquarters of the eponymous subdivision, block and tehsil in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located 95 km from Leh city on the old main road to Kargil, where it crosses the Indus over an iron bridge.

items.note: Temisgam is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.
Skinlingyong is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.
Nurla is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.
Tia is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Khalsi tehsil.

Domkhar (also spelt Damkhar) is a village in Sham district of Ladakh in India, located in the Khalsi tehsil. The Domkhar Rock Art Sanctuary was created in 2012, in order to preserve prehistoric petroglyphs in the area.
Hemis-Shukpachan or Hemishok Pachan is a village in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located on the right side bank of Indus River and is in the Likir tehsil.

Khalatse (Wylie: mkhar la rtse, THL: khar la tsé), often written as Khaltse or Khalsi, is the headquarters of the eponymous subdivision, block and tehsil in the Sham district of Ladakh, India. It is located 95 km from Leh city on the old main road to Kargil, where it crosses the Indus over an iron bridge.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tingmosgang monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tingmosgang monastery is Apr – Jun, Sep – Oct. Summers are 10–22°C and winters are -6–10°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tingmosgang monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (55 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (240 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tingmosgang monastery?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tingmosgang monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tingmosgang monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Tingmosgang monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tingmosgang monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Temisgam, Skinlingyong, Nurla, Tia, Leh, Domkhar.
